data.content:
YOLOs
YOLOs are a series of real-time object detection frameworks known for their balance between speed and accuracy.<SEP>YOLOs are a family of real-time object detection models known for their speed and efficiency.

DETRs
DETRs are end-to-end Transformer-based detectors that eliminate the need for Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS).<SEP>DETRs refers to a class of object detection models that use transformer architectures for end-to-end detection.

Yian Zhao
Yian Zhao is a researcher affiliated with Baidu Inc and Peking University, contributing to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Yian Zhao is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Wenyu Lv
Wenyu Lv is a researcher affiliated with Baidu Inc and Peking University, contributing to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Wenyu Lv is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Shangliang Xu
Shangliang Xu is a researcher affiliated with Baidu Inc, contributing to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Shangliang Xu is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Guanzhong Wang
Guanzhong Wang is a researcher affiliated with Baidu Inc, contributing to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Guanzhong Wang is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Jinman Wei
Jinman Wei is a researcher affiliated with Baidu Inc, contributing to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Jinman Wei is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Qingqing Dang
Qingqing Dang is a researcher affiliated with Baidu Inc, contributing to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Qingqing Dang is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Yi Liu
Yi Liu is a researcher affiliated with Baidu Inc, contributing to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Yi Liu is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Baidu Inc
Baidu Inc is a technology company based in Beijing, China, involved in the research and development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Baidu Inc is one of the affiliations of the authors of the research paper.

Jie Chen
Jie Chen is a researcher affiliated with Peking University, contributing to the development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Jie Chen is one of the authors of the research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection".

Peking University
Peking University is an academic institution in Shenzhen, China, involved in the research and development of RT-DETR.<SEP>Peking University is one of the affiliations of the authors of the research paper.

Real-Time DEtection TRansformer
RT-DETR is a real-time end-to-end object detector designed to address the limitations of YOLOs and DETRs.<SEP>Real-Time DEtection TRansformer is the full name of RT-DETR, an advanced object detection model.

COCO
COCO is a dataset used for evaluating object detection models, achieving 53.1% / 54.3% AP with RT-DETR.

T4 GPU
T4 GPU is a graphics processing unit used for testing model performance.<SEP>T4 GPU is a hardware platform used to evaluate the performance of RT-DETR, achieving 108 / 74 FPS.<SEP>T4 GPU is a hardware platform used to measure the inference speed of RT-DETR, achieving 108 FPS.

Objects365
Objects365 is a pre-training dataset used to enhance the performance of RT-DETR, achieving 55.3% / 56.2% AP.<SEP>Objects365 is a large-scale dataset used for pre-training RT-DETR, leading to improved performance.

Non-Maximum Suppression
NMS is a post-processing technique used in object detection that can slow down inference and introduce instability.<SEP>Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) is a limitation of YOLO detectors discussed in the research paper.

Transformer
Transformer is a neural network architecture used in DETRs for end-to-end object detection.<SEP>Transformer is the underlying architecture of the proposed RT-DETR model.

Hybrid Encoder
Hybrid encoder is an efficient design used in RT-DETR to process multi-scale features and improve inference speed.<SEP>The hybrid encoder is a component of RT-DETR designed to process multi-scale features efficiently.

Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection
Uncertainty-minimal query selection is a technique used in RT-DETR to select high-quality object queries based on localization confidence.<SEP>Uncertainty-minimal query selection is a technique used in RT-DETR to improve accuracy by selecting high-quality initial queries.<SEP>Uncertainty-Minimal Query Selection is a technique used in the RT-DETR model to reduce uncertainty in object detection.

CNN-Based Architecture
CNN-based architecture is a common approach in real-time object detection, exemplified by YOLOs.

Real-Time Object Detection
Real-time object detection is a research area with applications in autonomous driving, video surveillance, and more.<SEP>Real-Time Object Detection refers to the ability of a model to detect objects quickly and efficiently.

DINO-R50
DINO-R50 is a previous state-of-the-art model that RT-DETR outperforms in both accuracy and speed.

RT-DETR-R50
RT-DETR-R50 is a version of RT-DETR that achieves 53.1% AP on COCO and 108 FPS on T4 GPU.<SEP>RT-DETR-R50 is a variant of the RT-DETR model with a ResNet-50 backbone, achieving 53.1% AP and 108 FPS.

RT-DETR-R101
RT-DETR-R101 is a version of RT-DETR that achieves 54.3% AP on COCO and 74 FPS on T4 GPU.

RT-DETR
RT-DETR is a real-time end-to-end object detector proposed in the text, designed to improve speed and accuracy in object detection.<SEP>RT-DETR is a proposed Transformer-based solution for real-time object detection, introduced in the research paper.<SEP>RT-DETR is the proposed object detection model that achieves state-of-the-art performance according to Figure 1.<SEP>RT-DETR is a real-time end-to-end object detection model designed to improve accuracy and performance.

DETR
DETR is an end-to-end object detection model based on the Transformer architecture, which RT-DETR improves upon.<SEP>DETR is an object detection model that uses a transformer architecture for end-to-end detection.

Transformer Encoder
Transformer encoder is a component of the DETR model that processes features but is replaced in RT-DETR with a hybrid encoder.

YOLO Detectors
YOLO detectors are a family of one-stage real-time object detection models that RT-DETR outperforms in both speed and accuracy.<SEP>YOLO detectors are a class of object detection models that have limitations in terms of Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS).

COCO Val2017
COCO Val2017 is a benchmark dataset used for evaluating object detection models.<SEP>COCO Val2017 is a dataset used to evaluate the performance of RT-DETR, measuring its AP (Average Precision).

NMS
NMS (Non-Maximum Suppression) is a post-processing technique used in object detection to remove overlapping bounding boxes.<SEP>NMS is a post-processing algorithm used in object detection to eliminate overlapping output boxes by applying confidence and IoU thresholds.<SEP>NMS is a post-processing algorithm used in object detection to eliminate overlapping boxes, which RT-DETR eliminates.<SEP>NMS stands for Non-Maximum Suppression, a technique used in object detection to eliminate redundant detections.

AP
AP stands for Average Precision, a metric used to evaluate the accuracy of object detection models.<SEP>AP refers to Average Precision, a metric used to evaluate the accuracy of object detection models like RT-DETR.<SEP>AP stands for Average Precision, a metric used to evaluate the performance of object detection models.

FPS
FPS refers to Frames Per Second, a metric used to evaluate the speed of object detection models like RT-DETR.<SEP>FPS stands for Frames Per Second, a measure of how many frames a model can process in a second.

YOLOv1
YOLOv1 is the first CNN-based one-stage object detector that achieved real-time performance.<SEP>YOLOv1 is an early version of the YOLO object detection model, known for its speed.

Deformable-DETR
Deformable-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves training convergence with multi-scale features.<SEP>Deformable-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves efficiency through deformable attention mechanisms.

DINO-Deformable-DETR
DINO-Deformable-DETR is a previous state-of-the-art object detection model that RT-DETR outperforms.

L
L refers to a previously advanced YOLO detector model that RT-DETR outperforms in both speed and accuracy.

X
X refers to a previously advanced YOLO detector model that RT-DETR outperforms in both speed and accuracy.

S
S refers to a lighter YOLO detector model that scaled RT-DETRs outperform.

M
M refers to a lighter YOLO detector model that scaled RT-DETRs outperform.

DETR Variants
DETR variants include models like Deformable-DETR, DAB-DETR, DN-DETR, Group-DETR, Efficient DETR, Sparse DETR, Lite DETR, Conditional DETR, and Anchor DETR, each addressing specific limitations of the original DETR.

End-to-end Speed Benchmark
End-to-end speed benchmark is a method introduced to test the inference speed of real-time object detectors.

Group-DETR
Group-DETR is a variant of DETR that introduces group-wise one-to-many assignment to improve performance.

Efficient DETR
Efficient DETR is a variant of DETR that reduces computational cost by decreasing the number of encoder and decoder layers.

Lite DETR
Lite DETR is a variant of DETR that enhances encoder efficiency by reducing the update frequency of low-level features.

Sparse DETR
Sparse DETR is a variant of DETR that reduces computational cost by reducing the number of updated queries.

Anchor DETR
Anchor DETR is a variant of DETR that introduces anchors to improve query optimization.

Conditional DETR
Conditional DETR is a variant of DETR that decreases the optimization difficulty of queries.

DAB-DETR
DAB-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves performance through an iterative refinement scheme.<SEP>DAB-DETR is a variant of DETR that uses dynamic anchor boxes for improved detection.

DN-DETR
DN-DETR is a variant of DETR that improves performance through denoising training.

Query Selection
Query selection refers to techniques used to initialize high-quality object queries in DETR models.

Mixed Query Selection
Mixed query selection is a technique suggested by DINO to help better initialize queries in DETR models.

NMS Post-processing
NMS post-processing is a widely used algorithm in object detection to eliminate overlapping boxes, which RT-DETR eliminates.

YOLOv5
YOLOv5 is an anchor-based object detection model.<SEP>YOLOv5 is an anchor-based object detection model used for analysis in the text.<SEP>YOLOv5 is one of the object detection models compared in Figure 1.

YOLOv8
YOLOv8 is an anchor-based object detection model.<SEP>YOLOv8 is an anchor-free object detection model used for analysis in the text.<SEP>YOLOv8 is one of the object detection models compared in Figure 1.

COCO val2017
COCO val2017 is a benchmark dataset used for evaluating object detection models.

TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin
TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin is a specialized NMS implementation that includes multiple kernels for efficient non-maximum suppression.

FP16
FP16 refers to the 16-bit floating-point precision used in tensor operations for faster computation.

IoU Threshold
IoU threshold is a parameter used in NMS to determine when two bounding boxes are considered overlapping.

Confidence Threshold
Confidence threshold is a parameter used in NMS to filter out low-scoring prediction boxes.

NMS Execution Time
NMS Execution Time refers to the time required to perform non-maximum suppression on detected boxes.

End-to-End Speed
End-to-End Speed refers to the total time taken for a detector to process an input image from start to finish, including NMS.

EfficientNMS Kernel
EfficientNMS Kernel is a specific component of the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin responsible for non-maximum suppression.

Real-Time Detectors
Real-Time Detectors are object detection models designed to process images quickly for real-time applications.

EfficientNMSFilter
EfficientNMSFilter is a kernel within the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin used for filtering non-maximum suppression operations.

RadixSort
RadixSort is a kernel within the TensorRT EfficientNMSPlugin used for sorting operations during non-maximum suppression.

End-to-End Speed Benchmark
The End-to-End Speed Benchmark is a methodology for comparing the real-time performance of object detectors.<SEP>End-to-End Speed Benchmark is a method for evaluating the total processing time of real-time detectors, including NMS.

Appendix
Appendix refers to supplementary material that includes visualizations of YOLOv8 predictions under different NMS thresholds.

False Negatives
False Negatives are missed detections where the model fails to identify objects that are present.

False Positives
False Positives are incorrect detections where the model identifies objects that are not present.

TensorRT
TensorRT is a deep learning inference optimizer and runtime developed by NVIDIA.

YOLOv7
YOLOv7 is an anchor-based object detection model.<SEP>YOLOv7 is one of the object detection models compared in Figure 1.

PP-YOLOE
PP-YOLOE is an anchor-free object detection model.<SEP>PP-YOLOE is one of the object detection models compared in Figure 1.

YOLOv6
YOLOv6 is an anchor-based object detection model.

NVIDIA
NVIDIA is a technology company known for developing GPU and AI-related software and hardware.

EfficientNMSPlugin
EfficientNMSPlugin is a TensorRT plugin for optimizing Non-Maximum Suppression in object detection.

I/O
I/O refers to input/output operations, which are excluded from the end-to-end speed calculation in the benchmark.

MemoryCopy
MemoryCopy refers to memory copying operations, which are excluded from the end-to-end speed calculation in the benchmark.

Anchor-Free Detectors
Anchor-Free Detectors are a category of object detection models that do not rely on predefined anchor boxes for bounding box prediction.

Anchor-Based Detectors
Anchor-Based Detectors are a category of object detection models that use predefined anchor boxes for bounding box prediction.

This image is the first page of the academic paper introducing RT-DETR, a real-time end-to-end object detector. It features the title, author list, abstract, introduction, and Figure 1. Figure 1 is a critical performance comparison chart demonstrating that RT-DETR (red markers) outperforms existing YOLO series detectors (various colors) in both accuracy (COCO AP) and speed (latency) on a T4 GPU, validating the paper's central claim.

This image contains the left-hand text column of page 2 from the research paper introducing RT-DETR. It bridges the introduction and related work sections, detailing the technical innovations of the RT-DETR architecture (hybrid encoder, query selection) and its superior performance over YOLO and DINO models. The text also initiates a literature review on real-time and end-to-end object detectors, providing essential context for the study's contribution to computer vision.

data.entity_name: Page 3 of Research Paper on Object Detection Speed and NMS Analysis (image)
data.content: This image captures page 3 of a scientific paper analyzing the end-to-end speed of object detectors. It features a bar chart comparing box counts for YOLOv5 and YOLOv8 across different confidence thresholds, and a table detailing the impact of IoU and confidence thresholds on Average Precision (AP) and Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) execution time. The accompanying text discusses the computational costs of NMS, query initialization in DETR models, and establishes a benchmarking protocol using the COCO dataset.

Abstract
The abstract summarizes the proposal of RT-DETR and discusses the limitations of YOLO detectors regarding Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS).

Research Paper
The research paper titled "DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection" presents a new approach to real-time object detection using a Transformer-based model.

Figure 1
Figure 1 is a scatter plot comparing the performance of various object detection models, including RT-DETR.

Introduction
The introduction section of the research paper provides background information and outlines the problem with YOLO detectors.

End-to-end Latency T4 TensorRT FP16
End-to-end latency measured in milliseconds using T4 TensorRT FP16 is displayed on the X-axis of Figure 1.

COCO AP
COCO AP is a metric used to evaluate the performance of object detection models, displayed on the Y-axis of Figure 1.

YOLOv6-v3.0
YOLOv6-v3.0 is one of the object detection models compared in Figure 1.

R50 Scaled
R50 Scaled is one of the data points representing RT-DETR in Figure 1.

R18 Scaled
R18 Scaled is one of the data points representing RT-DETR in Figure 1.

R50
R50 is one of the data points representing RT-DETR in Figure 1.

Legend
The legend in Figure 1 provides information about the different models and their representations in the scatter plot.

R101
R101 is one of the data points representing RT-DETR in Figure 1.

Yolo Detectors
Yolo Detectors are a category of real-time object detection models known for their speed and efficiency.

Efficient Hybrid Encoder
Efficient Hybrid Encoder is a component of the RT-DETR model that enhances performance through optimized encoding.

Accuracy
Accuracy is a metric used to evaluate the effectiveness of object detection models.

Computational Redundancy
Computational Redundancy refers to unnecessary computations in object detection models that can be optimized.

End-to-End Object Detection
End-to-End Object Detection is a method where the entire detection process is handled by a single model.

DINO
DINO is a self-supervised object detection model that improves performance without labeled data.

Section 2
Section 2 is a part of the academic paper discussing related work on object detection models.

Real-Time Object Detectors
Real-Time Object Detectors are models designed to detect objects quickly and efficiently.

End-to-End Object Detectors
End-to-End Object Detectors are models that handle the entire detection process within a single framework.

Related Work
Related Work is a section in academic papers that reviews existing literature and prior research.

Academic Paper
An academic paper is a formal document presenting research findings, often published in journals or conferences.

Black Serif Text
Black Serif Text refers to the style of text used in the image, characterized by bold, clear lettering.

Scientific Publications
Scientific Publications are formal documents that present original research findings in scientific fields.

White Background
White Background is the color of the background in the image, providing contrast to the text.

Page 2
Page 2 is a specific page in the academic paper titled 'DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection.'

data.entity_name: DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection
data.content:
DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection
DETRs Beat YOLOs on Real-time Object Detection is the title of the academic paper discussing object detection models.

Speed Tuning
Speed Tuning refers to the adjustment of model parameters to balance performance and computational speed.

World Athletics Championship
The World Athletics Championship is a global sports competition featuring top athletes in track and field.

Tokyo
Tokyo is the host city of the World Athletics Championship.

Noah Carter
Noah Carter is a sprinter who set a new record in the 100m sprint at the World Athletics Championship.

100m Sprint Record
The 100m sprint record is a benchmark in athletics, recently broken by Noah Carter.

Carbon-Fiber Spikes
Carbon-fiber spikes are advanced sprinting shoes that provide enhanced speed and traction.

World Athletics Federation
The World Athletics Federation is the governing body overseeing the World Athletics Championship and record validations.

Authors
The authors contributed to the research paper on RT-DETR.

Image
Black Serif Text is the style of text used in the image, characterized by bold, clear lettering.
